channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Hamisi Nguku Completes Secondary School Using Feet Despite Disability
item.description: Hamisi Nguku, a young Kenyan born with hands that later became paralysed, has successfully completed his secondary education at Kilosa Secondary School. Despite his disability, he adapted to using his...

item.title: Pedestrian killed by Frontier Airlines plane during takeoff at Denver International Airport
item.description: A tragic incident occurred at Denver International Airport on Friday evening when a pedestrian was struck and killed by a Frontier Airlines Airbus A321 during its takeoff roll. The individual had jump...

item.title: Frontier Airlines Plane Strikes Pedestrian During Takeoff at Denver Airport, Prompting Evacuation
item.description: A tragic accident occurred at Denver International Airport on May 9, 2026, when a Frontier Airlines Airbus A321 struck a pedestrian during takeoff. According to airport authorities, the unidentified i...

item.title: Mini Crossword and Quiz Puzzles Available for Readers
item.description: The Sydney Morning Herald offers readers a range of interactive puzzles, including the Mini Crossword and Superquiz, providing an engaging way to test general knowledge and problem-solving skills. On ...

item.title: Zanzibar President Confirms Ongoing CCM-ACT Unity Government Discussions
item.description: Zanzibar President Hussein Mwinyi has publicly confirmed that CCM is in talks with opposition party ACT-Wazalendo about forming a Government of National Unity (GNU) following the recent general electi...

item.title: Philippines proposes AI hub to help MSMEs adopt advanced technologies
item.description: The Philippines has put forward a proposal to establish an Artificial Intelligence (AI) hub designed to assist micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs) in leveraging AI technologies for growth and...

item.title: Pakistan Armed Forces to Hold Official Ceremony at GHQ Marking One Year of Marka-i-Haq Victory
item.description: A special ceremony will be held at General Headquarters Rawalpindi on Sunday morning to mark one year since Pakistan achieved victory in Marka-i-Haq against India. According to Radio Pakistan, Field M...

item.title: EFCC Declares Former Minister Sadiya Farouq Wanted Over Alleged Fund Diversion
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) don declare former Minister of Humanitarian Affairs, Disaster Management and Social Development under Buhari administration, Sadiya Umar Farouq, wan...

item.title: Henrietta Lacks’ Family Reaches Confidential Settlement With Novartis Over HeLa Cells
item.description: Henrietta Lacks, born Loretta Pleasant in 1920, became a pivotal figure in medical research without her knowledge. In 1951, while being treated for aggressive cervical cancer at Johns Hopkins Hospital...

item.title: South Korean Temple Integrates Humanoid Robot into Buddhist Initiation Ceremony
item.description: A four-foot humanoid robot named Gabi has officially participated in a Buddhist initiation ceremony at a temple in Seoul, South Korea. During the sugye ceremony, which normally involves humans pledgin...

item.title: GWS Giants mock Essendon’s 20-year finals drought with pre-match banner in AFL clash
item.description: The GWS Giants stirred up controversy before their AFL match against Essendon by running through a cheeky banner that highlighted the Bombers’ long finals win drought. The banner featured a mock white...

item.title: VCK extends support to Vijay, easing Tamil Nadu government formation
item.description: The process of forming the new government in Tamil Nadu has seen a significant development. Actor-turned-politician Vijay's efforts to secure a stable majority were under scrutiny as numbers in the as...

item.title: Mouse Study Shows Brain Networks Start Dense and Refine Over Time
item.description: A recent study conducted by neuroscientists at the Institute of Science and Technology Austria (ISTA) challenges the traditional idea that the brain is a blank slate at birth. By examining the hippoca...

item.title: Premier Smith stresses dialogue with Ottawa as Alberta separatism rises
item.description: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith has emphasised the importance of dialogue with the federal government to address rising separatist sentiment in her province. Following her meeting in Ottawa with Prime ...

item.title: BBNaija winner Ilebaye’s father arrested after alleged domestic assault
item.description: The Federal Capital Territory Police in Abuja don arrest Emmanuel Odiniya, wey be the father of BBNaija winner Ilebaye Odiniya, after she report say him dey physically assault her. Ilebaye, wey dey 25...

item.title: World's 10 Largest Houses in 2026 Ranked by Size and Their Owners
item.description: This article lists the top 10 biggest houses in the world as of 2026, measured by total square footage including main buildings and other facilities. At number one is Istana Nurul Iman Palace in Brune...

item.title: La Salle Lady Spikers clinch UAAP women’s volleyball title with unbeaten 16-0 record
item.description: The La Salle Lady Spikers successfully completed an unbeaten 16-0 season as they defeated the NU Lady Bulldogs in straight sets to claim the UAAP Season 88 women’s volleyball championship. The final m...

item.title: Guardian writers reflect on the nostalgia of collecting World Cup Panini stickers
item.description: Panini World Cup sticker albums have been a cherished part of football culture for decades, but fans recently received the bittersweet news that the albums will be discontinued after 2030. Guardian wr...

item.title: European nations arrange special flights to safely evacuate passengers from hantavirus affected cruise ship in Canary Islands
item.description: Several European countries including Germany, France, Belgium, Ireland, and the Netherlands have decided to send special planes to evacuate their citizens from the MV Hondius cruise ship that has been...

item.title: Privacy Concerns Grow Over Meta Ray-Ban Smart Glasses AI Features and Data Annotation Practices
item.description: Meta's Ray-Ban smart glasses, powered by artificial intelligence, have seen significant commercial success, with over seven million units sold in 2025 alone. The devices feature built-in cameras and m...
